attacking_style: Not a primary attacker; role is sustained climbing support rather than launching late-race attacks. Operates as high-altitude pacemaker on final climbs.
team_tactics: Deployed as critical climbing domestique to control tempo and protect team leader on decisive mountain stages. Engine-room worker who sacrifices personal result for team objective.
terrain_preference: Best suited to sustained high-altitude climbing where diesel engine can set tempo. Exposed on punchy, short climbs or prolonged stage racing efforts.
vulnerability: Susceptible to being dropped on earlier climbs when form is suboptimal, leaving team leader exposed with significant distance remaining. Consistency across multi-day efforts appears variable.
key_patterns: Functions exclusively as high-mountain domestique for team leader, no personal ambitions evident (2 races).
Form fluctuates significantly: dropped early when off-form, massive climbing contributions when on-form (2 races).
Personal results sacrificed entirely for team tactics; no self-preservation instinct shown (2 races).
form_trajectory: positive: Dauphine performance showing massive climbing strength suggests peaked form heading into Tour, rebounding from earlier Paris-Nice struggles
